data.narrative:
EnerVenue, a Series B climate and energy company founded by Yi Cui, has raised over $400M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 peers—1 public company and 8 private entities—operating within the same sector and stage, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $400M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage step-ups, yielding $760M. The peer-median method examines six same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $355M and a range spanning $120M to $5.0B. The latter reflects significant heterogeneity within frontier-energy companies; Commonwealth Fusion Systems trades at the high end at $5.0B, while others cluster tighter around $300–500M.

The two methods triangulate to a median implied valuation of $558M, with a defensible range of $302M to $874M. The stage-progression approach supports the upper end, while peer-median anchors the lower bound. Key uncertainty stems from the wide dispersion in comparable valuations—a 41x spread from minimum to maximum peer valuation—reflecting inconsistent market pricing of early-stage climate-tech risk and technology maturity. Capital intensity and path-to-revenue visibility within the peer set materially influence individual valuations and warrant further due diligence.
